methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $626M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Black Forest Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $3.25B

data.narrative:
Poolside is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Jason Warner and Eiso Kant that has raised $626M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir, PLTR), including frontier AI and robotics companies at similar and adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $626M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $1.2B. Peer-median analysis examines 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C), Reflection AI ($25.0B, Series A), Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series A), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), and Together AI ($8.3B, Series A) among others—producing a median valuation of $14.0B and a range of $3.2B to $39.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous funding environments and company maturity within frontier AI.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.0B (low) to $16.1B (high), with a median of $7.6B. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer-median carries greater weight given direct comparability but reflects substantial uncertainty in frontier AI valuation multiples. Key risks include capital efficiency relative to peers, revenue traction visibility, and whether Poolside's positioning more closely mirrors early-stage ($8B–$15B) or later-stage ($14B+) companies in the comp set.
